mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly boosting equity valuations and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.5%, tight labor markets accelerate wage growth and consumer spending, driving corporate earnings upward and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 4.5%, or the signal's predictive power vanishes after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth combined with rising retail sales indicates robust aggregate demand outpacing supply constraints, which compresses profit margins less and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when INDPRO growth exceeds 2% but RSAFS growth remains flat, or cross-validation IC drops below 0.02 across three consecutive rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, rising short-term rates initially support bank profitability and credit availability, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days before valuation compression dominates.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns turn negative in > 60% of observations during consecutive 3-month FEDFUNDS hikes, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 50% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in housing starts exceeding 5% signals a sharp contraction in business and consumer confidence, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the HOUST drop, or the signal's predictive power vanishes after controlling for PAYEMS revisions.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls growth exceeds 200,000 while the unemployment rate simultaneously rises, it indicates a rapidly expanding labor force that suppresses wage inflation and supports soft-landing equity valuations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when PAYEMS growth exceeds 200k but UNRATE falls below 4.0%, or cross-validation IC drops below 0.02 across three consecutive rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and healthy business investment, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in months with positive INDPRO growth are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, accelerating real GDP growth combined with stable consumer spending drives earnings revisions higher, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day forward returns in > 55% of quarters where GDPC1 growth accelerates, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 50% in walk-forward tests.
